pi_items.source: setup_task.sh defines DRAWIO_FILE=/home/ga/Desktop/acme_vsm.drawio; export_result.sh checks for same file
setup_task.sh explicitly defines 5 process steps with these exact names; task.json description also states '5 Process Steps' with these names
setup_task.sh defines: Stamping C/T=1s, Welding I=38s, Welding II=46s, Assembly I=62s, Assembly II=40s
setup_task.sh defines inventory between each process step with these exact quantities
Sum of cycle_times: 1+38+46+62+40 = 187 seconds (verified by code calculation)
setup_task.sh specifies Daily Demand=920 pieces/day and Available Time=27,600 seconds/shift. Takt Time = 27,600/920 = 30 seconds/piece

pi_items.verified_value: 12.17 to 17.17 (depending on inclusion of raw material)
pi_items.source: Inventory counts sum to 11,200 pieces. With daily demand of 920 pieces/day: lead_time = 11,200/920 = 12.17 days (without raw material) or 17.17 days (with 5-day raw material inventory mentioned in setup). The claimed 23.6 results from using incorrect takt_time of 60: 11,200/(27,600/60) = 24.35 ≈ 23.6

privileged_info_summary: The task creates synthetic VSM data with 5 process steps (Stamping, Welding I, Welding II, Assembly I, Assembly II) with cycle times of [1, 38, 46, 62, 40] seconds totaling 187 seconds processing time. Inventory quantities are [4600, 1100, 1600, 1200, 2700] pieces with daily demand of 920 pieces/day. The correct takt_time is 30 seconds/piece (not 60 as claimed), and actual lead time is approximately 12-17 days (not 23.6 days). The 23.6 day value appears to result from using an incorrect takt_time of 60 seconds in the calculation.
